author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Arsenal Unpacked
subtitle: Main branch, 11:09 AM. He built the house before anyone knew he was moving in.
when_where: 🕐 11:09 AM on main, the daylight hours when the city expects productivity and honesty. The branch was pristine, untouched, a blank canvas waiting for structure. 🏗️ Omkar laid down the first stones: directories, packages, the skeleton of something that looked like order.
who_whom: 🔨 A developer and his own ambition locked eyes across the commit message. The codestory package wasn't just code; it was the blueprint for everything that would follow. 📐 He was building the cage before he knew what animal would live inside it.
what_why: ⚙️ He created the directory structure with surgical precision—src/codestory, the first confession in a language that wouldn't lie. Each folder was a room in a house designed for surveillance. 🧩 The package architecture was clean, modular, waiting to be filled with logic that would later spiral. 💀 By organizing the code, he organized his own descent. He didn't know it yet, but he'd just drawn the map of where he'd get lost.
verdict: He built the labyrinth before he understood he was the Minotaur.
